title: [CI:DOCS] Fix service_destinations description in podman man page
body:
- [service_destinations] should be [engine.service_destinations]
- service_destinations does not read from `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf` because podman uses config.ReadCustomConfig().

Fixes: #15615

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
> * because podman uses config.ReadCustomConfig().

This looks like a bug to me.  I dropped a FIXME on this issue in https://github.com/containers/podman/blame/main/cmd/podman/root.go#L580-L581 a while ago but we didn't explore it further yet.

Why is `machine` not reading from `/usr/share`.  Using `ReadCustomConfig()` `machine` will only read from the specified CONTAINERS_CONF env var or `/etc/containers`.  But it will not read any other file such as .d files or those in the home directory.

@ashley-cui @baude @n1hility  @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

> > * because podman uses config.ReadCustomConfig().
> 
> This looks like a bug to me. I dropped a FIXME on this issue in https://github.com/containers/podman/blame/main/cmd/podman/root.go#L580-L581 a while ago but we didn't explore it further yet.
> 
> Why is `machine` not reading from `/usr/share`. Using `ReadCustomConfig()` `machine` will only read from the specified CONTAINERS_CONF env var or `/etc/containers`. But it will not read any other file such as .d files or those in the home directory.
> 
> @ashley-cui @baude @n1hility @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

It is not a bug, it works exactly as intended. A rootless user cannot possible ever use connection that were created by root, the full overwrite logic does not apply here and would cause a lot of problems. As a user the connections are written to `~/.config/containers.containers.conf`. And if we write that file we should never clutter the local config file with a bunch of random defaults the we set internally. That means we have to read the file in its current for add our connection and then write it back. If we do anything else we will lock internal defaults for eternity which makes future changes extremely hard.

The big problem is that we decided to write to an existing config file in the first place. At the very minimum our writes will remove all comments.
We have support for  $HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf.d/*.conf We should move to writing a connections.conf file in that directory rather then rewriting the containers.conf file.

As you suggested, I think the best approach is to separate the service destinations section from `containers.conf` and move it to another file ( `connections.conf` ). I will fix according to this plan.
Changes LGTM as they reflect the current behavior.  Let's move the discussion how to improve (or not) out of this PR to unblock.
